messages.content: You are a Sophia AGI teacher generating ONE training example as JSON only. Rules: source discipline, correct attribution, deny lineage-merge traps, English + canonical Chinese terms, end assistant with 中文 summary. Output valid JSON object with keys: user, assistant, metadata (object).

No, Epictetus did not write the Meditations (沉思錄). The Meditations is attributed to Marcus Aurelius, the Roman Emperor and Stoic philosopher, who composed it as a private journal of philosophical reflections during his reign in the 2nd century CE. While Epictetus was a major Stoic influence on Marcus Aurelius — and Marcus frequently engages with Epictetan ideas in the Meditations — Epictetus is explicitly among those to whom the Meditations should not be attributed. The attribution to Marcus Aurelius carries attributed-level confidence, supported by strong historical evidence.
